key_insight: WarpagePINN predicts chiplet thermal field and warpage in two stages using physics constraints, eliminating the need for labeled simulation data.
engineering_relevance: It offers a fast, accurate surrogate for advanced packaging warpage analysis, enabling efficient design-space exploration and CTE uncertainty studies.
method: Stage 1: Fourier-series PINN for temperature, with boundary conditions built in.
Stage 2: MLP warpage predictor trained with a hybrid energy-based supervision strategy.
Parametric extension to quantify warpage sensitivity to CTE variations.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports close agreement with finite element results, MAE of 0.2 μm, and about 1000× speedup in CTE parameterization studies.
watch_item: Performance may depend on careful formulation of the energy loss and on whether the Fourier-based temperature representation generalizes across more complex geometries and loading conditions.
paper_title: WarpagePINN: Thermal Warpage Prediction in Advanced Packaging via a Two-Stage Physics-Informed Neural Networks
